title: atriplex hastata subsp. microsperma
intro: Atriplex hastata subsp. microsperma, also known as small-fruited saltbush, is a subspecies of the Atriplex hastata plant, a shrub native to Australia and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Atriplex hastata subsp. microsperma include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and act as a natural antiseptic, making it useful for treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of Atriplex hastata subsp. microsperma can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be used topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
